data.body:
B5 review-plan PASS (blocking=0, advisory=3), signed, claimed claude-b5. Advisory remediation:
- P9 (no file_impact): set_file_impact done (6 paths).
- T9 (flag lifecycle): verify.gate_engine flag — OWNER: rebar.toml [verify] section (operator); PRECEDENCE: standard config layering (user < project < REBAR_VERIFY_GATE_ENGINE env < CLI -c); DEFAULT: 'workflow' (this story cuts over); FALLBACK: 'bespoke' reachable until B-RETIRE (chip-rib-gong) removes the flag + bespoke run_review entirely. Documented in CLAUDE.md + config docstring.
- F1 (byte-compat deterministic check): AC2 verified by a golden-artifact test that runs the SAME verdict dict through both paths' signing and asserts the signed manifest is byte-identical — design: signing is UNCHANGED by this story (review_plan/transition.py signing wrappers untouched); only verdict PRODUCTION swaps (run_review vs workflow), so byte-compat is structural. Test: tests/unit/test_gate_engine_cutover.py asserts attest.build_manifest + _verdict_manifest are path-independent.

Design: dispatch verdict production inside review_plan()/verify_completion() on verify.gate_engine; signing/sidecar wrappers untouched → byte-compatible by construction. P1/P5/P8 reconcile: precheck only takes ELSE arm for exempt + P8-too-big (bespoke only stops LLM on P8); P1/P5 blocks flow det_blocking into decide. INDETERMINATE: catch LLMUnavailableError around the batch/workflow run. Dynamic verify: branch plan-review.yaml on code_grounded → agentic prompt vs single-turn.
